title: Wizard Power Development Process
rule: Four phases per wizard power, in order. Never skip ahead.

phases.name: Research
phases.goal: Understand the full landscape before writing a single line.
checklist: All viable providers — not just the obvious ones
Pricing model (per-use, subscription, tiered)
Sandbox / test environment availability
API maturity (stable vs beta, rate limits, uptime SLA)
Auth model (API key, OAuth, webhook, etc.)
Reversibility window — is there one? How long?
ToS restrictions (what's prohibited, what requires KYC/verification)
Compliance surface (legal, regulatory, fraud risk)
Real-world edge cases (what breaks in practice, not just in docs)
Existing MCP servers or Claude integrations (don't rebuild what exists)
phases.done_when: You can answer any question about the power without opening a browser.

phases.name: Prototype
phases.goal: Prove that the action can actually be taken end-to-end in a real environment.
checklist: Authenticate with the chosen provider
Execute the core action against the sandbox
Parse and print the response (confirmation ID, status, etc.)
Trigger at least one failure mode and confirm the error is readable
phases.done_when: Script has been run and output logged, including one error case.

phases.name: Write the Skill
phases.goal: Turn the prototype into a production-ready Claude skill.
checklist: Fill out all 8 dimensions using skill-anatomy.md template
Score 8/8 before moving to Phase 4
Wire the prototype's API logic into the skill's execution step
Define the confirmation gate copy exactly as it will appear to the user
Define the receipt format exactly as it will appear
phases.done_when: Score is 8/8 and skill file is committed.

phases.name: Test the Skill
phases.goal: Confirm the skill behaves correctly when invoked naturally.
checklist: Happy path — trigger with natural language, confirm action completes
Each failure mode from Phase 3 — trigger error condition, confirm message is clear
Cancellation path — if reversible, test cancel command within the window
Edge inputs — missing required fields, malformed addresses, etc.
Receipt format — confirm output is readable and contains everything needed to follow up
phases.done_when: All failure modes hit at least once, happy path confirmed, no surprises.

files.prototype-log.md: Phase 2 deliverable — actual run output proving it executed
